title: NYC Yellow Taxi Trip Analysis
subtitle: 3 million trips · January 2023
description: End-to-end data science project uncovering patterns in trip volume, fare pricing, tipping behavior, and revenue trends across NYC's yellow taxi fleet. Includes a linear regression model that predicts tip amounts with a Mean Absolute Error of $1.31.
highlights: Tuesday is the busiest day with ~491,000 trips
5am has the highest average fare due to airport runs
Credit card users tip an average of $4.30 vs $0 recorded for cash

title: U.S. Labor Market Analysis Pipeline
subtitle: ETL pipeline · 2022–2024
description: Fully automated ETL pipeline extracting data from BLS, FRED, and OEWS government APIs, transforming and loading into PostgreSQL, and visualizing key labor market trends across 809 occupations and 6 industry sectors.
highlights: Workers faced real purchasing power losses in early 2023 when inflation outpaced wage growth
Job openings fell below period average by September 2023
Database Architects lead data roles at $135,980 median annual wage
Pipeline orchestrated by a single pipeline.py script

title: Manchester United — The Premier League Trophy Drought Analysis
subtitle: 15 seasons · 2009/10 to 2023/24 · 10,000 Monte Carlo simulations
description: End-to-end data analytics project investigating why Manchester United have not won the Premier League since Sir Alex Ferguson retired in 2013. Covers performance decline, manager impact, transfer efficiency, and a Monte Carlo simulation model that estimates the probability and timeline of United's next title challenge.
highlights: Average points dropped from 85 (Fergie era) to 66 (post-Fergie) — a 19 point fall
Champions gap grew from 1 point behind to 35 points behind in a single decade
No post-Fergie manager has hit the 2.0 PPG title challenger threshold
Transfer spend shows no consistent correlation with league finish
Monte Carlo model gives United a 24% chance of winning the PL in the next 10 years
